title: The Cinnamon Potion and the Ant-Sized Tree
summary: After visiting Seeker's magical castle, Pooki the Pig and his friends sample a mysterious cinnamon-scented potion. A mishap with the teleportation rhyme—turned into a series of coughs—shrinks the group to the size of ants. Stranded on a bouncy green leaf high in a tree, they must avoid hungry birds and navigate the massive branches. They eventually return home using a special potion, ending their day with lemon ginger tea and a relaxing puzzle.

highlightQuote.text: Potions are something you drink and then you say a rhyme and then they teleport you somewhere. That's how they work.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what are we doing now?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to know what we're doing now! Oh, no!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, I think Isabella knows what's happening. What's happening?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Pooki says, what's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, that, that's when we tell a story under stars. When we turn off the light you can, I'll pick you up and you, so you can see it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ooh, they're so beautiful, says Pooki. But where does the story come from?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The story, we tell it by ourselves.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and can I listen in?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh, yeah. Yes, you can repeat every part we said. Oh. Ding-dong, Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now, now Pooki the Pig, now you say that, just what I said. And what Daddy said.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, ding-dong, Seeker, who is it? says Pooki.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's Victor!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hello Victor, I haven't seen you in a while, says Seeker. Come on in. Now, I'd like to introduce you to my new friend whose name is Pooki the Pig. And Pooki said, I'd like to introduce you to my new friend whose name is Pooki the Pig. And what'd Victor say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: My name is Victor the Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Pooki said, You know, I'm new around here. I don't really know how anything works. Um, can you show me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, do you know what that closet over there is for? It's for potions.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Potions? What are those?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Potions are something you drink and then you say a rhyme and then they teleport you somewhere. That's how they work.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! Like, like where? To the library or something?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, to different kinds of places. To places you've never been before.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That sounds amazing! I mean, I've never been to the swimming pool. Is that what you mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of place?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like, like Greenland Shark World.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Greenland Shark World? That sounds like a place I should get away.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But, well, but it's dangerous. It's very dangerous because leopard seals. If you go too far down, then a leopard seal might bite you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ooh, scary. What, what about the Greenland Shark?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, they might bite you too. But Dad, we need to turn on the light for just one second.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, why's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because my toenail needs to be cutted.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Pause Stars and Stories and do a little toenail trim. Oh, this part of it, I see the problem.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, Daddy, Amy's clippers are the best clippers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we don't have those right now. Let me try these and we'll see how it goes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, I got it, I got it. I got a little bit. I think I can get the rest, I think.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, it's better to use the clipper, but okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can just pick at it, pick at it, pick at it. Got it. Got it fine.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's start our story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, back to Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where's Pooki the Pig? I need the stuffed Pooki the Pig.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Pooki's pig says, umph, umph! Oh, you were sitting on me!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sorry.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Seeker, I think Pooki the Pig wants to go on a potion.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker says, Oh, I see. Well, we'd better take Pooki...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pooki the Pig, which potion do you want? You can come see the options. Seeker, can you lead Pooki the Pig to your potion closet, please?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker led Pooki the Pig to the potion closet. And he said, well, you know, Isabella just built me a really nice new castle with a new potion closet that actually has new Lego potion shelves in it. And because of that, I reorganized everything, I threw out some old potions, I made some new ones, and I put everything away on the new shelves. But actually, I forgot to label them, so I don't know where anything is. And so we just got to try something and find out. We might have to just take a mystery potion and see where we end up. It could take us someplace totally new. You never know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like that swirly light green one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, says Seeker. There is a swirly light green one right here. I never noticed that one. I wonder what that'll do. Here, have a sniff and tell me what it smells like.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It smells like cinnamon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A swirly light green potion that smells like cinnamon. And Pooki, if you want, you can do the honors, and make up a little rhyme, and we'll say it three times after we each take a sip, and that will take us. Okay, sip, sip, sip. So Seeker took a sip, and Pooki took a sip... Oh, was there someone else with them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No one. No one else.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just Seeker and Pooki. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Just Seeker and Pooki and me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and Isabella. So Seeker and Pooki and Isabella took a sip of the potion, and Pooki said the rhyme. Okay, go ahead.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But instead of me saying the rhyme, what, because I was coughing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, somehow this time, the magic went sideways, and because of the coughing, the rhyme just became cough-a-cough, cough-ka-cough, cough-ka-cough! And then... (sound effects)... they found themselves in a mysterious new place.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (laughs)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Pooki the Pig looked around and said, Whoa! Where are we? And Seeker said, now this is strange. A potion's never taken me to a world like this. And he looked down at his feet, and it was just a smooth flat green surface. But he pressed on it a little bit, and it was a little bit bouncy. And he looked up, and he saw what looked like a tree top up above him. And he said, this is very strange. Huh. Oh, wait a minute, he said as he peeked over the edge of the green flat surface they were sitting on, and he saw down below, far below, was the ground. And he said, Isabella, something amazing has happened. Pooki, something amazing has happened. We are in a world where I believe that we are standing in a regular-sized tree, but we're standing on one of the leaves, which normally would be very small compared to us, but we in this world are very small. We're the size of ants! We've been made teeny-tiny! My goodness! What should we do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think you should nestle up for this part. Yes, you should nestle up for the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Pooki the Pig and Isabella nestled up together on the leaf, which was actually quite cozy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now, now I'm nestling up in the chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I see. In the chair, yes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Seeker, we listen to the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Seeker said, I wonder if we should try to get down from this tree, because I hear some birds chirping far away. And if they see us, they might come down and try to investigate, and they might think that we're bugs and try to eat us. So, let's hide. Hmm. Maybe we should try to get onto the branch so we can get to the tree trunk where the bark is, and then maybe we could start to climb down. Will you come with me, Isabella and Pooki?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, um, I think we still have to nestle up in the chair because I see something that... I just want to finish our story. Daddy?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's something that's wrong. All the stars are kind of... All the stars are, like, on the door. They're usually on the door, but now they're not.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Should we just move them over a little bit?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And Daddy?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, Pooki says, what are those potions in your pocket, Seeker?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, those are my going home potions.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I feel like I'm so excited to show you something that I brought, that I left at your castle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, all right, said Seeker, we can go back home.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, so I can show you what I brought.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Good idea. And so Seeker and Isabella and Pooki sipped the going-home potion, and then poof, they were right back in Seeker's cozy living room once again.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So, and Pooki said, do you see that bag with those animals? Here's, here's a puzzle of a garden that has a tree in the middle. It's a puzzle of trees and flowers.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I love puzzles, said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a puzzle of trees and flowers, and I think and it's really tricky. So if you can help me do it, then I would be delighted because I haven't done it in a while. And I just brought it up from the basement.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And so Pooki and Isabella and Seeker sat at the living room table in Seeker's castle, and they emptied out the puzzle and sorted out all the puzzle pieces and started to put them together. And that was very relaxing. Seeker enjoyed doing puzzles and he said, would anyone like some tea while we do our puzzle?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, please, said Pooki the Pig.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What kind of tea would you like, Pooki?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Lemon ginger tea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, coming right up. And so Seeker and Isabella and Pooki passed a quiet evening...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I brought some tea!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you brought some of that as well. Uh, drinking their tea and doing the puzzle. And pretty soon, they had finished the puzzle and it had gotten a little bit late, and so it was time for everyone to go home and go to sleep. And that's the end of our story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (silly noises)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Nice bird.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, that's a butterfly.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, nice butterfly, yes. I like it. All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy...
